articles.title: Kevin Durant’s Availability for Game 2 Remains Unclear
articles.original_title: When Will Kevin Durant Return? Latest on Rockets Star’s Game 2 Status - heavy.com
articles.summary: Heavy.com reports that the Houston Rockets are still evaluating Kevin Durant’s health ahead of Game 2 of the playoffs. No official timeline has been given for his return, and the Rockets will decide based on his recovery progress.
key_points: Durant’s status for Game 2 has not been confirmed.
The Rockets are monitoring his condition and will announce a timeline when possible.
His potential return could impact the Rockets’ offensive strategy against the Lakers.

key_points: A revised return schedule for Luka Dončić has been communicated to the Lakers.
The update may influence the Lakers’ game plan against Houston.
Dončić’s health remains a key factor in the Mavericks’ playoff outlook.

articles.title: Luka Doncic Snubbed for Second Consecutive NBA Awards
articles.original_title: Lakers Star Luka Doncic Disrespected With 2nd NBA Awards Snub - heavy.com
articles.summary: Luka Doncic was again left off the NBA Awards finalist list, marking his second straight snub. The omission sparked criticism from fans and analysts who view the star as one of the league’s top performers.
key_points: Doncic was not named a finalist for any major NBA award this season.
This is the second year in a row he has been excluded despite strong statistical production.
The snub has prompted debate over the award voting criteria and potential bias.

articles.title: Luka Dončić Fails to Reach MVP Finalist Cut
articles.original_title: Luka Dončić not named finalist for 2026 NBA MVP award
articles.summary: Although Luka Dončić qualified for the MVP race via the Extraordinary Circumstances Challenge, he was not among the three finalists announced on Sunday. The finalists are Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander, Nikola Jokić, and Victor Wembanyama.
key_points: Dončić was eligible but not selected as an MVP finalist.
The final three candidates are SGA (OKC), Jokić (DEN), and Wembanyama (SA).
The decision highlights the competitive nature of the MVP race this season.

Star players delivered clutch performances that will influence future matchups.
Injuries and bench depth proved decisive factors in several series.

articles.title: Lakers' Luka Doncic Excluded from NBA MVP Finalist List

articles.summary: Sports Illustrated reports that Luka Doncic was not among the three finalists for the NBA MVP award, joining Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander, Nikola Jokić, and Victor Wembanyama as the only players recognized. The omission adds to ongoing discussion about award voting transparency.
key_points: Finalists: SGA (OKC), Jokić (DEN), Wembanyama (SA).
Doncic’s exclusion follows a similar snub in previous award cycles.
Analysts question whether voting criteria fully reflect regular‑season impact.

articles.title: Luka Doncic Misses NBA MVP Finalist Spot

articles.summary: Lakers Nation confirms that the MVP finalists for the 2025‑26 season are Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander, Victor Wembanyama, and Nikola Jokic, leaving Luka Doncic off the list despite his strong season performance. The news underscores the competitive field for the league’s top individual honor.
key_points: MVP finalists: Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander (OKC), Victor Wembanyama (SA), Nikola Jokic (DEN).
Doncic’s season statistics remain among the league’s best, yet he was not selected.
The result fuels ongoing debate about the MVP voting process.

articles.title: Derek Anderson Criticizes Mark Pope’s Kentucky ‘Mentality’ Approach
articles.original_title: Derek Anderson gets brutally honest on why Mark Pope is struggling to teach the Kentucky ‘mentality’
articles.summary: Former Kentucky star Derek Anderson told KSR that the group chat with Mark Pope’s 1996 championship teammates is far more intense than Pope admits, suggesting it hampers his ability to instill the desired Kentucky mindset. Anderson implies the constant pressure from former players is a key factor in Pope’s coaching struggles at Kentucky.
key_points: Anderson says the group chat with Pope’s former teammates is highly demanding.
He believes this pressure prevents Pope from effectively teaching the "Kentucky mentality".
The comments highlight internal challenges within Pope’s coaching staff.

articles.title: LeBron James, 41, Still Leads Lakers in Playoffs
articles.original_title: LeBron James is 41. And he’s somehow still carrying his team in the playoffs
articles.summary: Despite being 41, LeBron James has taken on a primary scoring and leadership role for the Los Angeles Lakers in the postseason, largely due to injuries to key teammates. The veteran’s performance defies expectations for a player at his age, keeping the Lakers competitive.
key_points: LeBron is carrying the Lakers offensively in the playoffs.
Injuries to other stars have forced him into a larger role.
His production remains elite for a player over 40.

articles.title: Chet Holmgren Named Defensive Player of the Year Finalist
articles.original_title: NBA Announces Chet Holmgren as Defensive Player of the Year Finalist
articles.summary: The NBA announced that rookie center Chet Holmgren has been selected as a finalist for Defensive Player of the Year, marking the first time a Gonzaga alumnus has been in contention for a major league award. Holmgren’s impact on the defensive end has been highlighted throughout his inaugural season.
key_points: Holmgren joins the elite defensive shortlist for his first NBA season.
He is the first Gonzaga player ever to be a finalist for a major NBA award.
His shot‑blocking and rim‑protection have drawn league‑wide attention.

articles.title: Victor Wembanyama Scores 35 in Spurs Playoff Debut
articles.original_title: Wemby tops Duncan with 35 in Spurs playoff debut
articles.summary: Victor Wembanyama recorded 35 points, the most ever by a San Antonio Spurs player in a postseason debut, leading the team to a 111-98 victory over the Portland Trail Blazers in Game 1 of the first‑round series. His performance set the tone for the Spurs’ early series momentum.
key_points: Wembanyama’s 35 points set a franchise record for a playoff debut.
The Spurs defeated the Trail Blazers 111-98 in Game 1.
His scoring and defensive presence were pivotal to the win.

articles.title: Orlando Magic Stun Top‑Seeded Detroit Pistons in Game 1
articles.original_title: 'We belong here': Magic stun Pistons in Game 1
articles.summary: The Orlando Magic delivered a surprising upset over the No. 1‑seed Detroit Pistons in Game 1 of their playoff series, showcasing a level of intensity and fight that had been lacking during the regular season. The victory positions the Magic as a legitimate contender in the matchup.
key_points: Magic defeated the top‑seeded Pistons in Game 1 of the series.
The win highlighted a newfound intensity and resilience from Orlando.
The upset shifts momentum early in the playoff contest.

key_points: Celtics start the series with a convincing win.
Jayson Tatum led Boston with a standout performance.
Philadelphia faces its seventh straight series defeat to Boston.

They plan to play golf together during the offseason.

Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander and Nikola Jokic also remain in contention.
The finalists reflect standout individual contributions this season.

articles.title: Thunder dominate Suns in Game 1, expect Phoenix to improve
articles.original_title: Thunder take Game 1, expect Suns 'to get better'
articles.summary: Defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder opened their postseason with a 119‑84 rout of the Phoenix Suns at the Paycom Center. Despite the loss, Thunder coach indicated the Suns are likely to adjust and become more competitive in the series.
key_points: Thunder win Game 1 by a 35‑point margin.
Phoenix Suns were held to 84 points, their lowest output of the season.
Thunder anticipate the Suns will make adjustments and improve in upcoming games.

articles.title: NBA Community Calls Luka Dončić MVP Snub ‘Malpractice’ and ‘Insane’
articles.original_title: 'Malpractice,' 'Kind of Insane' -- NBA World Reacts as Lakers Star Luka Dončić Is Snubbed as MVP Finalist - PFSN
articles.summary: Following the release of the MVP finalist list, Luka Dončić was omitted despite his historic statistical season, prompting a wave of criticism across the league. Players, analysts, and fans described the exclusion as a major oversight and questioned the voting process.
key_points: Dončić was not named among the final MVP candidates.
Reactions on social media labeled the decision “malpractice” and “insane.”
Debate centered on the criteria used by voters versus Dončić’s record‑breaking numbers.

articles.title: LeBron James Prepares for Post‑NBA Media Role, Partner Says
articles.original_title: LeBron James is getting ready for his post-NBA media career, partner says - Semafor
articles.summary: LeBron James is actively planning his career after retirement, focusing on media and entertainment projects, according to his partner. The discussion highlighted his growing involvement in production, commentary, and brand partnerships as he nears the end of his playing days.
key_points: James is building a media pipeline that includes TV, streaming, and film work.
His partner confirmed that these plans are already in motion while he still plays.
The move reflects a broader trend of NBA stars transitioning to media roles after retirement.

articles.title: NBA MVP Finalists Announced: Luka Doncic Snubbed Despite Historic Season
articles.original_title: NBA MVP Finalists revealed: Luka Doncic snubbed despite historic season - BasketNews.com
articles.summary: The league released its official MVP finalist roster, and Luka Doncic was notably absent even after posting one of the most impressive seasons in recent memory. The omission sparked discussion about the voting criteria and the weight given to team success versus individual performance.
key_points: Finalist list did not include Doncic, who posted career‑high averages.
Analysts pointed to Dallas’ playoff positioning as a possible factor.
The snub reignited conversation about how MVP voters value advanced metrics.

articles.title: Luka Doncic’s MVP Chances Shift After 65‑Game Rule Relief
articles.original_title: Lakers' Luka Doncic's MVP hopes take sudden turn after 65-game rule relief - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: A recent NBA rule adjustment granting players who miss 65 games due to injury a chance at award consideration has altered Luka Doncic’s MVP outlook. The change could make him eligible despite missing games, reviving his candidacy late in the season.
key_points: NBA introduced a 65‑game eligibility relief rule for award voting.
Doncic missed several games, but the rule may keep him in MVP conversation.
The development adds uncertainty to the final MVP voting landscape.
